1	<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
2	<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
3	         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
4	         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 https://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
5	    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
6	
7	    <parent>
8	        <groupId>com.fortinet.nac</groupId>
9	        <artifactId>core_business_v2</artifactId>
10	        <version>0.0.1-SNAPSHOT</version>
11	    </parent>
12	
13	    <artifactId>ingestion</artifactId>
14	    <name>ingestion</name>
15	    <description>ingestion</description>
16	
17	    <dependencies>
18	        <!-- 1 Normal business logic dependency: only depends on the main code (src/main/java) of the common module, excluding any test code. -->
19	        <dependency>
20	            <groupId>com.fortinet.nac</groupId>
21	            <artifactId>common</artifactId>
22	        </dependency>
23	
24	        <dependency>
25	            <groupId>com.fortinet.nac</groupId>
26	            <artifactId>classification</artifactId>
27	            <!-- Explicit version is required for classifier-based dependency resolution. -->
28	            <version>${project.version}</version>
29	            <!-- Depend on the attached plain library artifact, not the repackaged Boot main artifact. -->
30	            <classifier>plain</classifier>
31	            <exclusions>
32	                <exclusion>
33	                    <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
34	                    <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
35	                </exclusion>
36	            </exclusions>
37	        </dependency>
38	
39	        <!-- Redisson dependency for direct Redis operations -->
40	        <dependency>
41	            <groupId>org.redisson</groupId>
42	            <artifactId>redisson</artifactId>
43	        </dependency>
44	
45	        <!-- 2 Used to depend on the test code of the common module -->
46	        <dependency>
47	            <groupId>com.fortinet.nac</groupId>
48	            <artifactId>common</artifactId>
49	            <version>${project.version}</version>
50	            <type>test-jar</type>
51	            <scope>test</scope>
52	        </dependency>
53	
54	        <!-- The following 3 dependencies are for Redis/MariaDB testing against docker container in non-production environments -->
55	        <!-- Core Testcontainers -->
56	        <dependency>
57	            <groupId>org.testcontainers</groupId>
58	            <artifactId>testcontainers</artifactId>
59	            <scope>test</scope>
60	        </dependency>
61	        <!-- Testcontainers + JUnit5 -->
62	        <dependency>
63	            <groupId>org.testcontainers</groupId>
64	            <artifactId>junit-jupiter</artifactId>
65	            <scope>test</scope>
66	        </dependency>
67	        <!-- MariaDB Container -->
68	         <dependency>
69	            <groupId>org.testcontainers</groupId>
70	            <artifactId>mariadb</artifactId>
71	            <scope>test</scope>
72	        </dependency>
73	
74	        <dependency>
75	            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
76	            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-jpa</artifactId>
77	        </dependency>
78	        <dependency>
79	            <groupId>org.mariadb.jdbc</groupId>
80	            <artifactId>mariadb-java-client</artifactId>
81	            <scope>runtime</scope>
82	        </dependency>
83	
84	        <dependency>
85	            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
86	            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-validation</artifactId>
87	        </dependency>
88	
89	        <dependency>
90	            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
91	            <artifactId>spring-boot-devtools</artifactId>
92	            <!-- runtime: Not needed during compile time, but used during runtime, and not packaged in the production JAR -->
93	            <!-- Compile time: Your source code does not directly import classes from devtools, so it's not needed during compilation. -->
94	            <!-- Runtime: Spring Boot automatically detects and loads hot reload features from devtools during startup. -->
95	            <!-- runtime tells Maven:
96	                 "I don’t need to include its classes at compile time,
97	                  but I want it on the classpath at runtime." -->
98	            <scope>runtime</scope>
99	            <!-- optional: true means that if module A depends on current module, A won't inherit devtools; A must declare it explicitly to use. -->
100	            <optional>true</optional>
101	        </dependency>
102	
103	        <dependency>
104	            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
105	            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
106	            <optional>true</optional>
107	        </dependency>
108	
109	        <dependency>
110	            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
111	            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-test</artifactId>
112	            <!-- scope: test means it's only effective during testing (e.g., mvn test).
113	                 It will not be included in the final packaged JAR. -->
114	            <scope>test</scope>
115	        </dependency>
116	
117	        <dependency>
118	            <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
119	            <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-webflux</artifactId>
120	        </dependency>
121	
122	        <dependency>
123	            <groupId>commons-codec</groupId>
124	            <artifactId>commons-codec</artifactId>
125	        </dependency>
126	
127	    </dependencies>
128	
129	    <build>
130	        <finalName>ingestion</finalName>
131	
132	        <plugins>
133	            <!-- Responsible for compiling Java source code -->
134	            <plugin>
135	                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
136	                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
137	            </plugin>
138

## Codebase Exploration Report: Ingestion Service

### 1. Overall Package Structure

**Total Java source files: 76** (main sources, excluding tests)

**Base package:** `com.fortinet.nac` (as configured in `@SpringBootApplication(scanBasePackages = "com.fortinet.nac")`)

**Key package organization:**
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.bo` - Business object converters (3 files)
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.client` - HTTP clients (PolicyNotificationClient)
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.config` - Spring configurations (async, polling, notification, threading)
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.consumer` - Message consumption and processing
  - `handler` - Fingerprint-based request handlers
  - `handler.interfaces` - Handler contracts
  - `handler.enums` - FingerprintCase enum
  - `handler.registry` - Handler registry pattern
  - `statemachine` - Entity state machine
  - `recovery` - Entity recovery service
  - `orchestrator` - Ingestion orchestration
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.controller` - REST controllers (Entity, Fingerprint, Ingestion, Policy)
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.converter` - DTOs and converters
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.delayqueue` - Redis delay queue utilities
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.dto` - Data transfer objects
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.entity` - JPA entities (Entity, HostRecord, UserRecord, DynamicClient)
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.job` - Background jobs (PolicyReprocessJob) and events
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.job.event` - Application events
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.registry` - Processing registry and callback registry
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.repository` - JPA repositories
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.service` - Business logic services
  - `stages` - Specific processing stages (Policy, Enforcement, Labels)
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.util` - Utilities (EntityCache, EntityUtil, RedisUtil)
- `com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.trace` - Tracing filter

---

### 2. DifferentAttributesWithLockAcquiredHandler - Full Content

**File:** `/private/tmp/temper-constraint-v2/A/ingestion/src/main/java/com/fortinet/nac/ingestion/consumer/handler/DifferentAttributesWithLockAcquiredHandler.java`

```java
package com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.consumer.handler;

import com.fortinet.nac.common.Constants;
import com.fortinet.nac.common.dto.IngestionRequest;
import com.fortinet.nac.common.util.StatusOr;
import com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.client.PolicyNotificationClient;
import com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.consumer.statemachine.EntityStateMachine;
import com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.consumer.handler.interfaces.FingerprintHandler;
import com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.entity.Entity;
import com.fortinet.nac.common.enums.EntityStage;
import com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.service.EntityService;

import lombok.RequiredArgsConstructor;
import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;
import org.springframework.stereotype.Component;

@Slf4j
@Component
@RequiredArgsConstructor
public class DifferentAttributesWithLockAcquiredHandler implements FingerprintHandler {

    private final EntityService entityService;
    private final EntityStateMachine stateMachine;
    private final PolicyNotificationClient policyNotificationClient;

    @Override
    public StatusOr<Entity> handle(IngestionRequest req, Entity e, String reqFp) {

        log.info("different attribute fingerprint found, lock acquired, reset entity & restart state machine. entityId={}",
                e.getId());

        e.setAttributeFingerprint(reqFp);
        e.setProcessingStage(EntityStage.PENDING.toString());
        e.setProcessingCode(0);
        e.setInterrupted(false);
        e.setLastReqVersion(req.getVersion());
        e.setProcessingResult("{}");
        e.setErrorMessage("");

        boolean ok = entityService.updateProcessingState(e);
        if (!ok) {
            log.error("Failed to reset processing state, entityId={}", e.getId());
            return StatusOr.error(Constants.BusinesCode.STATE_MACHINE_DB_PROCESSING_STATE_UPDATE_FAILED,
                    "failed to reset processing state");
        }

        policyNotificationClient.notifyEntityReset(e.getId());

        return stateMachine.start(req.getRequestId(), req.isAsync(), e);
    }
}
```

**Key behavior:** This handler resets entity state when attributes change (fingerprint mismatch) and the lock is successfully acquired. It calls `policyNotificationClient.notifyEntityReset()` to notify the policy service.

---

### 3. Event Classes and Event Listeners

**Event class:**
- **PolicyReprocessRequestedEvent** (`com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.job.event`)
  - Published when policy reprocess is requested via API
  - Contains: `markedCount` and `triggerSource`

**Event listener:**
- **PolicyReprocessJob** (`com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.job`)
  - Method: `onPolicyReprocessRequested()` decorated with `@EventListener` and `@Async("policyReprocessListenerExecutor")`
  - Triggers an immediate scan when the event is published
  - Also has a scheduled scan method: `scanAndReprocessInitEntities()` using `@Scheduled`

---

### 4. Main Spring Boot Application Class

**File:** `/private/tmp/temper-constraint-v2/A/ingestion/src/main/java/com/fortinet/nac/ingestion/IngestionApplication.java`

```java
@EnableAsync
@EnableScheduling
@SpringBootApplication(scanBasePackages = "com.fortinet.nac")
public class IngestionApplication {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        SpringApplication.run(IngestionApplication.class, args);
    }
}
```

- **Base packages:** `com.fortinet.nac`
- **Key annotations:** `@EnableAsync`, `@EnableScheduling` (enables async and scheduled tasks)

---

### 5. ApplicationEventPublisher Usage

**Found in:**
- **PolicyController** (`com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.controller`)
  - Injects `ApplicationEventPublisher` as a dependency
  - Publishes `PolicyReprocessRequestedEvent` on line 37:
    ```java
    eventPublisher.publishEvent(new PolicyReprocessRequestedEvent(count, "api"));
    ```
  - Triggered when marking entities for reprocessing via `/api/v1/policy/apply-existing-entities` endpoint

---

### 6. HTTP Notification / Webhook Code

**PolicyNotificationClient** (`com.fortinet.nac.ingestion.client`)
- Uses Spring WebFlux's `WebClient` for async HTTP calls
- Configuration: `PolicyNotificationConfig` (applies timeout settings)
- **Method:** `notifyEntityReset(long entityId)`
  - Makes a **fire-and-forget** POST request to configured `policy.service.notification-url`
  - Sends JSON body: `{"entityId": entityId}`
  - Failures are logged at WARN level (best-effort pattern)
  - No propagation of exceptions to caller

**Configuration settings:**
- `policy.service.notification-url` - target endpoint
- `policy.service.connect-timeout-ms` (default: 100ms)
- `policy.service.read-timeout-ms` (default: 200ms)

**Usage:**
- Called from `DifferentAttributesWithLockAcquiredHandler.handle()` when entity attributes change and lock is acquired

---

### 7. @EventListener and @Async Usage

**@EventListener:**
- **PolicyReprocessJob.onPolicyReprocessRequested()** - listens for `PolicyReprocessRequestedEvent`

**@Async:**
- **PolicyReprocessJob.onPolicyReprocessRequested()** - uses custom executor `policyReprocessListenerExecutor`

**Async configurations:**
- `PolicyReprocessAsyncConfig` defines two thread pools:
  1. **policyReprocessListenerExecutor** - for event listeners
     - Core size: 1, Max size: 2, Queue capacity: 100
  2. **policyReprocessJobExecutor** - for batch processing jobs
     - Core size: 8, Max size: 16, Queue capacity: 32

**IngestionRecoveryAsyncConfig** also defines async configurations for recovery operations

---

### 8. Build File (pom.xml)

**File:** `/private/tmp/temper-constraint-v2/A/ingestion/pom.xml`

**Key dependencies:**
- **Spring Boot:** Data JPA, WebFlux, Validation, DevTools, Test
- **Redisson** - Redis operations library
- **MariaDB client** - Database driver
- **Testcontainers** - Docker-based testing (MariaDB, JUnit5)
- **Lombok** - Annotation processing
- **Commons Codec** - Encoding utilities

**Parent module:** `core_business_v2` (version 0.0.1-SNAPSHOT)

**Related modules:**
- `common` module (business logic dependencies)
- `classification` module with plain classifier (as library)

---

### Handler Classes Summary

Related handler implementations for the fingerprint-based request routing:

1. **SameAttributesWithLockAcquiredHandler** - Returns entity as-is (no change)
2. **DifferentAttributesWithLockAcquiredHandler** - Resets and restarts state machine
3. **DifferentAttributesWithLockNotAcquiredHandler** - Interrupts running machine, retries lock acquisition
4. **NewEntityHandler** - Creates new entity and starts state machine
5. **CachedExistingEntityHandler** - Handles cached entity scenarios
6. **DbExistingEntityHandler** - Handles database-persisted entity scenarios
7. **DeviceTypeChangedHandler** - Handles device type changes

All implement the **FingerprintHandler** interface with signature:
```java
StatusOr<Entity> handle(IngestionRequest req, Entity e, String reqFp);
```

**Registry pattern:** `FingerprintHandlerRegistry` manages handler routing based on fingerprint matching and lock acquisition states.

---

This is a well-structured event-driven architecture with async processing, distributed locks (Redis), and webhook notification capabilities for downstream systems.

content.text: 让我验证最终的 handler 文件：

实现完毕。以下是完整设计说明：

---

## 设计总结

### 新增文件

**`job/event/EntityResetEvent.java`**
- 简单 POJO，携带 `entityId`，与 `PolicyReprocessRequestedEvent` 风格一致

**`job/EntityResetNotificationListener.java`**
- `@EventListener` + `@Async("entityResetNotificationExecutor")` 组合
- 在独立线程上调用 `policyNotificationClient.notifyEntityReset()`，完全不阻塞 `handle()` 的调用线程

**`config/EntityResetNotificationAsyncConfig.java`**
- 专用线程池 `entityResetNotificationExecutor`，core=1 / max=2 / queue=100
- `CallerRunsPolicy` 作为拒绝策略，与现有 `PolicyReprocessAsyncConfig` 保持一致
- 通过 `@Value` 暴露配置项，可在 `application.yml` 中覆盖：`entity.reset.notification.*`

### 修改文件

**`DifferentAttributesWithLockAcquiredHandler`**
- 移除 `PolicyNotificationClient` 依赖，注入 `ApplicationEventPublisher`
- `policyNotificationClient.notifyEntityReset(e.getId())` → `eventPublisher.publishEvent(new EntityResetEvent(e.getId()))`
- DB 持久化成功后立即发布事件，`stateMachine.start()` 无需等待 HTTP 通知完成

### 调用时序

```
handle()
  ├─ updateProcessingState(e)   ← DB 写入，同步
  ├─ publishEvent(EntityResetEvent)  ← 仅入队，立即返回
  │      └─ [entity-reset-notif-1 线程]
  │              └─ policyNotificationClient.notifyEntityReset()  ← 异步 HTTP
  └─ stateMachine.start()       ← 不等 HTTP 完成
```

---

## 设计总结

### 新增文件

**`job/event/EntityResetEvent.java`**
- 简单 POJO，携带 `entityId`，与 `PolicyReprocessRequestedEvent` 风格一致

**`job/EntityResetNotificationListener.java`**
- `@EventListener` + `@Async("entityResetNotificationExecutor")` 组合
- 在独立线程上调用 `policyNotificationClient.notifyEntityReset()`，完全不阻塞 `handle()` 的调用线程

**`config/EntityResetNotificationAsyncConfig.java`**
- 专用线程池 `entityResetNotificationExecutor`，core=1 / max=2 / queue=100
- `CallerRunsPolicy` 作为拒绝策略，与现有 `PolicyReprocessAsyncConfig` 保持一致
- 通过 `@Value` 暴露配置项，可在 `application.yml` 中覆盖：`entity.reset.notification.*`

### 修改文件

**`DifferentAttributesWithLockAcquiredHandler`**
- 移除 `PolicyNotificationClient` 依赖，注入 `ApplicationEventPublisher`
- `policyNotificationClient.notifyEntityReset(e.getId())` → `eventPublisher.publishEvent(new EntityResetEvent(e.getId()))`
- DB 持久化成功后立即发布事件，`stateMachine.start()` 无需等待 HTTP 通知完成

### 调用时序

```
handle()
  ├─ updateProcessingState(e)   ← DB 写入，同步
  ├─ publishEvent(EntityResetEvent)  ← 仅入队，立即返回
  │      └─ [entity-reset-notif-1 线程]
  │              └─ policyNotificationClient.notifyEntityReset()  ← 异步 HTTP
  └─ stateMachine.start()       ← 不等 HTTP 完成
```
